items.title: Ancient DNA reveals the mysterious collapse of Europe's megalith builders
items.summary: DNA from a 5,000-year-old French megalithic tomb reveals that the people buried before and after a population collapse were genetically unrelated, pointing to a major migration after a devastating crisis. The shift coincided with new social traditions and the disappearance of the communities that bu

items.title: Harvard scientists turn a silicon chip into a DNA writing machine
items.summary: Scientists have created a silicon chip that can write dozens of DNA sequences simultaneously using electricity and water-based enzymes, offering a cleaner alternative to conventional DNA manufacturing. The breakthrough could eventually support portable DNA-writing devices and even massive DNA data s

items.title: Curiosity Sees Martian Sulfur Up Close
items.summary: This close-up view shows fragments of sulfur crystals — the first ever seen on the Red Planet. The crystals were found after NASA’s Curiosity Mars rover happened to drive over a rock and crush it on May 30, 2024. Several days later, Curiosity used a camera on the end of its robotic arm to take [&#82

items.title: This Mars rover could finally reveal whether life ever existed on Mars
items.summary: The hunt for ancient life on Mars just got an important test run. Scientists confirmed that the Rosalind Franklin rover's sophisticated instrument can detect subtle differences in two stable molecules that could preserve evidence of past life for billions of years. items.title: A vitamin A discovery is changing what scientists know about vision
items.summary: A surprising discovery is reshaping scientists' understanding of how humans develop sharp central vision before birth.
